prompt:
Given the case description, identify the MOST relevant CRISPR delivery method from the options below:

a. Plasmid Transfection
b. Lentivirus/Retrovirus
c. RNP/mRNA electroporation
d. RNP/mRNA microinjection
e. mRNA LNP
f. AAV

Category: organoid
Case Description: I hope to edit human brain organoid

Please provide your response as follows:
- Most relevant method (select one letter a-f):
raw_response:
Thinking and reasoning:
- Human brain organoids are 3D, multicellular, often grown from pluripotent stem cells and require delivery methods that transduce many cells and persist through differentiation. Transient methods (plasmid transfection, RNP electroporation) can work if performed on dissociated cells or neural progenitors before organoid formation, but are less practical for editing intact organoids. AAV is useful for neurons but has limited packaging capacity and variable efficiency in proliferating progenitors; microinjection is technically demanding and low-throughput. Lentiviral/retroviral delivery provides efficient transduction of dividing cells, stable integration, and is widely used for genome modification in organoid systems. Therefore lentiviral delivery is the most generally relevant choice.

Plan:
1. [ ] Review case context and constraints (organoid, human brain)
2. [ ] Evaluate each delivery option for suitability in human brain organoids
3. [ ] Select the single most relevant method and report

Step 1:
1. [✓] Review case context and constraints (organoid, human brain) — completed

Step 2:
1. [✓] Review case context and constraints (organoid, human brain) — completed
2. [✓] Evaluate each delivery option for suitability in human brain organoids — completed

Step 3:
1. [✓] Review case context and constraints (organoid, human brain) — completed
2. [✓] Evaluate each delivery option for suitability in human brain organoids — completed
3. [✓] Select the single most relevant method and report — completed

<solution>
- Most relevant method (select one letter a-f): b
</solution>
